pr.title: Bring over libc matches from OoT-GC
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Imported a large batch of Metrowerks libc/MSL decompilation work from OoT-GC into Melee. The PR replaced the hand-written assembly stub for direct_io fwrite with matching C, added substantial printf/ansi_fp implementations, reorganized MSL headers and source-file boundaries, introduced libc-specific compiler flags, and updated GALE01 splits/symbols to match the new MSL layout. The decomp.dev report recorded +7320 matched-code bytes overall, including 100% matches for fwrite and many printf helpers, while noting some functions moved between files rather than net-disappeared.
what_changed: Deleted asm/MSL/direct_io.s and added src/MSL/direct_io.c containing a C implementation of fwrite; configure.py changed MSL/direct_io.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Added a new Libc helper in configure.py with libc-specific cflags: -use_lmw_stmw on, -str pool,readonly, -common off, and -inline deferred, with fix_epilogue disabled for the MSL library.

Replaced the MSL library configuration from RuntimeLib to Libc and updated object entries: direct_io became Matching, cstring was split into mbstring.c and mem.c, misc_io.c was inserted, and console_io.c was renamed/configured as uart_console_io.c.

Added large printf.c coverage with parse_format, long2str, longlong2str, round_decimal, float2str, __pformatter, __FileWrite, __StringWrite, vprintf, vsnprintf, vsprintf, and sprintf. The object remained marked NonMatching, but the bot report showed many individual printf functions newly at 100%.
Added ansi_fp.c and ansi_fp.h, including _fpclassify, bit_values, digit_values, decimal/decform definitions, and __num2dec support used by float formatting.

Removed src/MSL/ansi_files.h and src/stdio.h, replacing/centralizing FILE, stdio enums, __OutStrCtrl, __InStrCtrl, stdin/stdout/stderr, fwrite, sprintf, vprintf, and vsprintf declarations in src/MSL/stdio.h.
Moved src/stdarg.h to src/MSL/stdarg.h and simplified it around the Metrowerks-style __va_list, __builtin_va_info, __va_arg, va_start, va_arg, and va_end definitions.

Reworked src/MSL/string.c: renamed local constants MSL_String_804D5B58/MSL_String_804D5B5C to K1/K2, added/reordered strlen, strcpy, strncpy, strcmp, strncmp, strchr, and __StringRead, and kept the word-aligned null-byte detection tactic in strcpy/strcmp.

Split former cstring responsibilities into src/MSL/mbstring.c for wcstombs and src/MSL/mem.c for memchr, memcmp, and memmove; GALE01 splits were updated from MSL/cstring.c to MSL/mbstring.c plus MSL/mem.c.
Added src/MSL/misc_io.c with an empty __stdio_atexit function, and shifted the printf split start from 0x80323CF0 to 0x80323CF4 accordingly.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to rename local sdata symbols MSL_String_804D5B58 and MSL_String_804D5B5C to K1 and K2.

Adjusted trigf.c substantially: added __sinit_trigf_c initialization of __four_over_pi_m1, used f32 signatures for sinf/cosf, and kept wrappers around sinf/cosf; configure.py still marked trigf.c NonMatching.
Changed .github/workflows/build.yml so the Upload report step runs for diff mode on success or failure: matrix.mode == 'diff' && (success() || failure()).
Made small include/path or naming fallout fixes in Dolphin/Runtime files per changed-file list, but the diff slice provides little detail beyond one-line edits.
smart_moves: Ported known OoT-GC libc matches wholesale instead of solving each MSL function in isolation, which yielded a large matched-code gain quickly for shared Metrowerks library code.

Introduced a dedicated Libc build configuration rather than forcing MSL through the generic RuntimeLib flags; this preserved libc-specific codegen knobs such as lmw/stmw use, readonly string pooling, no common symbols, deferred inline behavior, and no epilogue fixer.

Respected original object boundaries by splitting cstring into mbstring, mem, and misc_io and by renaming console_io to uart_console_io in both configure.py and splits.txt. This avoided treating moved functions as lost matches.
Centralized FILE and stdio-related declarations in src/MSL/stdio.h after removing ansi_files.h and root src/stdio.h, reducing duplicate/competing header definitions.
Used symbol renames K1/K2 to encode the actual local constant names used by MSL string code, improving future symbol searchability over address-derived placeholders.
Kept printf.c as a NonMatching object even while many helper functions matched individually, which avoids overstating full-object status while still banking partial decomp progress.
Updated CI artifact upload to run on failed diff builds, making match-regression reports easier to retrieve when the build or diff job fails.
decomp_lessons: Shared Metrowerks libc code from sibling GameCube decomp projects can be highly reusable, but it must be paired with Melee-specific object splits, symbols, headers, and compiler flags.

When functions move between source files, the decomp.dev report may show decreases in old file/function buckets and increases in new buckets. Here wcstombs, memcmp, memchr, memmove, __stdio_atexit, and console I/O appear as losses under old names and 100% matches under new files.

For MSL matching, correct headers are part of the match: FILE layout, bitfield enum names, stdarg ABI definitions, wchar_t handling, and stdin/stdout/stderr macros all affect compile output and callsites.

Do not assume a big source import means the whole object matches. printf.c gained many 100% helper matches but stayed Object(NonMatching), and trigf.c remained NonMatching despite __sinit_trigf_c matching.
Libc codegen may need its own flags instead of game/runtime defaults. In this PR, -use_lmw_stmw on and -inline deferred are explicitly tied to MSL matching.
Address-derived temporary symbols should be replaced when source-library names are known; K1/K2 are more useful and more likely source-accurate than MSL_String_804D5B58/MSL_String_804D5B5C.
Object split files and GALE01 splits.txt must move together. Adding mbstring.c, mem.c, and misc_io.c required exact text ranges and shifted printf.c's start address.
naming_conventions: Use MSL source filenames that mirror library modules: direct_io.c, mbstring.c, mem.c, misc_io.c, uart_console_io.c, ansi_fp.c, printf.c, trigf.c.

Use MSL-style internal lowercase/underscore enum names in stdio.h, such as __read, __write, __closed_file, __console_file, __unoriented, __char_oriented, __wide_oriented, __neutral, __writing, __reading, and __rereading.
Use FILE field names mode, state, buffer_len, saved_buffer_len, buffer_pos, position_proc, read_proc, write_proc, close_proc, and idle_proc in the new stdio.h layout.
Use __OutStrCtrl fields CharStr, MaxCharCount, and CharsWritten, and __InStrCtrl fields NextChar and NullCharDetected.
Prefer source-meaningful local constant names K1 and K2 for MSL string word-scanning masks over address-derived placeholders.
Moved stdarg header into the MSL include namespace as src/MSL/stdarg.h with guard _STDARG_H_; new stdio.h uses guard _STDIO and string.h uses _STRING_H_.
The console I/O module is named uart_console_io.c in splits/config rather than the older console_io.c label.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the 210-line PowerPC assembly file asm/MSL/direct_io.s after replacing __fwrite/fwrite behavior with C in src/MSL/direct_io.c.
MSL fwrite matching relies on FILE bitfields and buffer fields, fwide orientation handling, __prep_buffer, __flush_buffer, memcpy, and an empty __stdio_atexit stub in misc_io.c.

The string routines use word-aligned copying/comparison and the classic null-byte detection mask constants K1 = 0x80808080 and K2 = 0xfefefeff; preserving this structure helps match Metrowerks optimized string code.
The dedicated libc flags include -use_lmw_stmw on, which is relevant for matching functions that save/restore multiple registers with lmw/stmw patterns like the removed direct_io assembly did.

The printf implementation decomposes formatting into smaller helpers: parse_format for spec parsing, long2str/longlong2str for integer conversion, float2str plus __num2dec for floating conversion, and __pformatter with WriteProc callbacks for file/string output.
ansi_fp.c includes a comment noting a float ordering issue near __num2dec; exact floating-point expression ordering is a known matching sensitivity.
trigf.c initializes __four_over_pi_m1 in __sinit_trigf_c and uses explicit f32 sinf/cosf signatures and fabsf__Ff checks, but bot output indicated sinf/cosf were still only partial after this PR.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report: matched code increased to 26.18% (+0.19%, +7320 bytes) and linked code to 19.59% (+0.02%, +736 bytes).
The bot report confirmed direct_io fwrite reached 100% (+732 bytes).

The bot report confirmed many printf helpers reached 100%, including sprintf, vprintf, __StringWrite, __FileWrite, __pformatter, float2str, round_decimal, longlong2str, long2str, and parse_format; vsprintf remained partial at 46.43%.
The bot report showed moved functions reached 100% under new modules: wcstombs in mbstring, memcmp/memchr/memmove in mem, __stdio_atexit in misc_io, and console I/O functions in uart_console_io.
The bot report showed trigf sinf/cosf remained partial and lower-scoring after the changes, while __sinit_trigf_c reached 100%. No reviewer rationale was provided.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that make src/MSL/printf.c Object(Matching) or improve vsprintf from 46.43%.
Look for later PRs that finish trigf.c sinf/cosf matching after this partial restructuring.
Check whether follow-up cleanup consolidated remaining root libc headers after stdarg.h moved into src/MSL.
Search for other OoT-GC libc port PRs to reuse cflags_libc and MSL header layouts consistently.
Audit whether Dolphin/Runtime one-line changes in this PR were include-path fallout from moving stdio/stdarg headers.
